SMB owners have low NPS around tax services; don't refer their CPAs
Partners and SMB owners showed strong early demand signals
AI tools dramatically accelerate prototyping (10-20x) and refactoring with good test coverage

Embracing the 'pain and uncertainty' of building from scratch
Critical decision: knowing when to use AI vs. traditional approaches
AI excels at: prototyping, refactoring, internal tools; less helpful for: PRD writing, estimation
Some technical debt now less concerning due to faster refactoring capability
Actively using: Cursor, Claude, Grok, Granola, Clay
Values UI/UX quality as differentiator (Granola's phone UI)
Prefers tools with less 'political correctness' filtering

extraction.email_draft_ready:
**Subject: The Vibe Podcast - Town Founder on Building SMB Tax Software & Modern Development with AI**

Hi,

John Weissman, founder of Town, shared fascinating insights on this week's episode of The Vibe about why he started a business tax preparation software company and how AI tooling is reshaping development velocity.

**Key Takeaways:**

• **Market Opportunity**: No one has productized business tax preparation the way TurboTax did for personal taxes. Small business owners have low NPS around their tax services and don't refer their CPAs—indicating a real pain point.

• **Founder Motivation**: John was drawn to the challenge and the opportunity to work directly with SMB owners he describes as "mini founders"—people running dentistry practices, corner stores, and service businesses who chose entrepreneurship over corporate jobs.

• **AI-Assisted Development**: He's shipping 4,000 lines of code in 1.5 weeks (~10 hours/week) using AI tools like Cursor and Claude. The biggest gains: rapid prototyping (10-20x faster) and refactoring work with good test coverage.

• **Critical Caveat**: The real speedup comes from prototyping and getting customer feedback faster—not from moving vibe-coded prototypes directly to production. At Dropbox, John noted teams spent more time *deciding* what to build than actually building it.

• **Tool Stack**: Cursor + Claude for personal workflows; also uses Grok, Granola, and Clay. He values tools with strong UI/UX and less filtering.

**Next Steps:**

If you're building with AI tools or exploring tax software for SMBs, this conversation offers practical lessons on balancing speed with quality and knowing when AI actually helps (vs. creates busywork).

Enjoy the full episode on YouTube or your favorite podcast platform.

Best,
The Vibe Team
